data.text: I’ve written the evidence files. I’m doing one last targeted check to confirm `deadcode` still reports no `internal/evidence` symbols before I hand this back as a blocked run.

Blocked before commit.

- I recorded the reachability decisions in [decisions.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ae4b7393-20260506T072449-2488a616/.ddx/executions/20260506T072449-2488a616/decisions.md).
- I also wrote the required no-change blocker note in [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ae4b7393-20260506T072449-2488a616/.ddx/executions/20260506T072449-2488a616/no_changes_rationale.txt).
- `deadcode` is clean for `internal/evidence`: `cd cli && go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./... | rg 'internal/evidence'` returned no matches.
- The repo-level gate is red: `cd cli && go test ./...` fails in unrelated `cmd` tests, including `TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI` and `TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier`.

I stopped without a commit because fixing those failures would require out-of-scope edits. If you want, I can take the `cmd` test failures as a separate follow-up bead.
